Description: Angular 17+ default; no NgModule needed
Do: Standalone components for all new code

Description: Signals are Angular's reactive primitive for fine-grained reactivity
Do: Signals for component state over class properties

Description: Built-in control flow syntax replaces *ngIf/*ngFor directives
Signal-based inputs/outputs replace @Input()/@Output() decorators
Do: input() and output() for component API

Description: ng-content for flexible component composition
Do: ng-content with select for named slots
Don't: Rigid templates that can't be customized

Description: Single responsibility; components should do one thing
Do: Extract sub-components when template exceeds 50 lines

Description: Reduces re-renders by only checking on input changes or signal updates
Use renderer or ElementRef sparingly; prefer template bindings
Load route chunks on demand to reduce initial bundle

Guideline: Use route guards with functional API
Description: Protect routes with canActivate/canMatch functional guards
Do: Functional guards returning boolean or UrlTree

Description: Pre-fetch data before route activation using resolve
Don't: Fetching data in ngOnInit causing flash of empty state
Code Bad: Fetch in ngOnInit with loading state flickering

Description: Use inject(ActivatedRoute) with signals or toSignal
Compose shared layouts using router-outlet nesting
Do: Nested routes with shared layout components
Code Bad: Duplicate header/sidebar in each route component

Description: Preload lazy modules in background after initial load
Signals provide synchronous reactive state without RxJS overhead
Code Good: const items = signal<Item[]>([]); addItem(i: Item) { this.items.update(arr => [...arr i]) }

Description: Lazily evaluated derived values that update when dependencies change
Do: computed() for values derived from other signals
Code Good: readonly total = computed(() => this.items().reduce((s i) => s + i.price 0))

Description: Effects run side effects when signals change; avoid overuse
Do: effect() for side effects like logging or localStorage sync
Don't: effect() for deriving state (use computed instead)

Guideline: Use NgRx Signal Store for complex state
Description: NgRx Signal Store is the modern lightweight state management for Angular
Don't: Full NgRx reducer/action/effect boilerplate for simple state
Code Good: const Store = signalStore(withState({ count: 0 }) withMethods(s => ({ increment: () => patchState(s { count: s.count() + 1 }) })))

Description: Services with signals share state across components without a store
Do: Injectable service with signals for cross-component state
Don't: Prop drilling or @Input chains for shared state

Guideline: Avoid mixing RxJS and signals unnecessarily
Description: Use toSignal() to bridge RxJS into signal world at the boundary
Do: toSignal() to convert observable to signal at component edge
Don't: Subscribing in components and storing in signal manually

Description: FormGroup/FormControl with explicit generics for compile-time safety
Reactive forms scale better and are fully testable
Don't: FormsModule with ngModel for complex forms

Description: Functional validators are composable and tree-shakeable
Code Good: const noSpaces: ValidatorFn = ctrl => ctrl.value?.includes(' ') ? { noSpaces: true } : null

Description: Control when validation runs to avoid per-keystroke validation overhead
FormArray manages variable-length lists of controls
Code Good: get items(): FormArray { return this.form.get('items') as FormArray }

Description: Use form control touched and dirty states to show errors at the right time
Do: Show errors after field is touched
Don't: Show all errors on page load

Description: OnPush + signals eliminates most unnecessary change detection cycles
Don't: Default strategy which checks entire tree on every event

Description: Stable identity for list items prevents full DOM re-creation on change
Defer blocks lazy-load components when they enter the viewport
Do: @defer with on viewport for non-critical UI
Don't: Eagerly loading all components at startup

Description: Enforces image best practices: lazy loading LCP hints and proper sizing
Don't: Plain img tags for CMS or user content

Description: Import only what you use from Angular packages
Don't: Import BrowserAnimationsModule when not using animations

Description: Subscriptions leak and cause bugs; prefer async pipe or toSignal
Do: toSignal() or async pipe instead of manual subscribe
Don't: Manual subscribe without unsubscribe in ngOnDestroy

Description: Pre-render pages for faster LCP and better SEO
Do: SSR or SSG for public-facing routes
Code Bad: // no SSR, client renders empty shell

Description: Standalone components + provideRouter() eliminate dead NgModule code
TestBed sets up Angular DI for realistic component testing
Harnesses provide a stable testing API that survives template refactors
Don't: Direct native element queries that break on template changes

Description: Spectator wraps TestBed with a cleaner API reducing test setup noise
Isolate unit tests by providing mock implementations of dependencies
Do: SpyObj or jest.fn() mocks for services
Don't: Real HTTP calls in unit tests
Code Bad: providers: [UserService] // real service in unit test

Description: Test full route navigation including guards and resolvers
Don't: Mock all routing behavior in unit tests
Code Good: const harness = await RouterTestingHarness.create(); await harness.navigateByUrl('/home')

Description: Signals update synchronously; no async flush needed in most cases
Do: Read signal value directly in test assertions
Don't: TestBed.tick() or fakeAsync for signal reads

Description: Default emulation scopes styles to component preventing global leaks
Do: Emulated or None for intentional global styles

Description: Style the component's host element using :host pseudo-class
Don't: Adding wrapper div just for styling

Guideline: Use CSS custom properties for theming
Description: CSS variables work across component boundaries and enable dynamic theming
Do: CSS custom properties for colors and spacing
Don't: Hardcoded hex values in component styles

Description: Tailwind utilities work alongside Angular's ViewEncapsulation via global stylesheet
Do: Add Tailwind in styles.css and use utility classes in templates
Don't: Custom CSS for layout that Tailwind already handles

Description: Material 3 uses design tokens for systematic theming
Do: M3 token-based theming for Angular Material
Don't: Overriding Angular Material CSS with deep selectors

Description: Provide configuration via InjectionToken for testability and flexibility
Code Good: const API_URL = new InjectionToken<string>('apiUrl'); provide: [{ provide: API_URL useValue: env.apiUrl }]

Description: Intercept requests for auth headers error handling and logging
Don't: Service-level header management in every request
Code Bad: httpClient.get(url { headers: { Authorization: token } }) in every call

Description: Feature-based folder structure scales better than type-based
Do: Feature folders with collocated component service and routes

Description: Separate environment values for dev staging and prod via Angular build configs
Don't: Hardcoded API URLs or feature flags in source

Description: inject() function is composable and works in more contexts than constructor injection
Code Good: readonly http = inject(HttpClient); readonly router = inject(Router)
Code Bad: constructor(private http: HttpClient private router: Router) {}
